summary: Deprecated audit trail for the raw power-manager performance bundle. The current app bundles several low-level registry values into one preset, but the publishable control surface is not a single Microsoft-supported toggle.

setting.name: Low-Level Power Performance Bundle
setting.casual_explanation: This tweak tries to reduce several power-saving behaviors at once to favor raw performance and responsiveness.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want an aggressively performance-focused system and are willing to give up power efficiency.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This bundle can reduce some power-saving behavior, but it also increases the chance of heat, battery drain, or tuning side effects. The review problem is that the bundle is built from low-level raw registry values rather than a supported Microsoft preset surface.
setting.professional_notes: The app writes CoalescingTimerInterval, DeepIoCoalescingEnabled, EventProcessorEnabled, LatencyToleranceParked, LatencyToleranceSoftParked, and EnergyEstimationEnabled under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power. Repo notes track these values, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft registry source validating the combined bundle as a supported configuration preset.

targets.value_name: CoalescingTimerInterval + DeepIoCoalescingEnabled + EventProcessorEnabled + LatencyToleranceParked + LatencyToleranceSoftParked + EnergyEstimationEnabled

allowed_values.label: Primary-source mapping not confirmed
allowed_values.meaning: The bundle is based on low-level power-manager values, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft registry source that validates the exact preset.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes a broad performance-focused registry bundle, but this review did not elevate that raw bundle to a primary-source-backed Windows preset.

writes.value_name: CoalescingTimerInterval + DeepIoCoalescingEnabled + EventProcessorEnabled + LatencyToleranceParked + LatencyToleranceSoftParked + EnergyEstimationEnabled

windows_defaults.label: Windows-managed power baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using normal power-manager behavior

states.rationale: This review does not publish a raw bundled registry baseline for these low-level values.

windows_defaults.notes: Normal Windows power behavior should be treated as the baseline until a supported configuration surface is confirmed.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that should keep supported Windows power behavior
avoid_for: None until a supported Microsoft mapping is confirmed

states.rationale: Keep the stock Windows-managed power baseline.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want supported Windows power behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are trying to force a bundle of undocumented low-level power-manager overrides.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its normal power-management behavior.
The app's raw bundle remains documented but not validated.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes a six-value performance-focused bundle under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power.

evidence.title: Repo power notes
evidence.summary: The repo tracks these low-level values as research notes and reverse-engineered observations, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft registry source for publishing the bundle as validated.

decision.why: This is a broad raw power-manager registry bundle without a primary Microsoft-supported preset mapping in this review.
blocking_issues: No primary Microsoft registry mapping was found for the exact raw bundle used by the app.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Repo power notes: The repo tracks these low-level values as research notes and reverse-engineered observations, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft registry source for publishing the bundle as validated.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id repo-power-doc (repo-doc); deprecated audit trail.
